title: Wood Slat Low Shelving Unit
summary: A short, wide, open shelving unit on stubby 2x4-and-1x3 leg sets — long 1x4 shelf boards with an even center gap, sized for under a window or as toy/book storage.
description:
This is a low, wide shelving unit rather than a tall bookcase — short leg sets keep the whole unit at a height that works under a window or alongside a low bed, with three full-length 1x4 shelf boards running the unit's whole width. Build the leg sets first from 1x3s and 2x4 supports, and attaching the outside shelf boards first (rather than starting in the center) gives you a fixed, level reference to build the rest of the shelf against.

Use a level as you attach each shelf board — checking level shelf by shelf, rather than trusting the leg sets alone, is what keeps a wide, low unit like this from developing a lean across its length. The center boards go on last, with an even, deliberate gap left between them and the side boards — the source notes this is a design choice, not a mistake to fix, so don't force the boards tight together.

Optional 1x2 cross supports on the back add real rack resistance for very little material — lay them across the back, mark, and cut angled ends to fit rather than trying to calculate the angle. One cross support was enough on the original build, but add a second for a longer or more heavily loaded unit.

tools.note: Cross support angle cuts; a handsaw works too.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit at an angle.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the 1x3 legs to the 2x4 supports with 2" wood screws and glue, two screws per joint.

steps.title: Attach the outside shelf boards
steps.body: Attach the shelving boards on the outsides first, to the outside legs, using a level to keep the legs level as you attach shelving.

steps.title: Attach the center boards
steps.body: Attach the center boards last, leaving an even, deliberate gap between the center boards and the side boards.

steps.title: Add cross supports and finish
steps.body: Lay the optional cross supports across the back and mark for cutting. Cut the angled ends with a miter saw or handheld saw and attach to the back. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Low, wide open shelving unit with slatted shelves
